key_moments.description: Investigator informs the mother that the crash is a homicide investigation.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: What we're looking at this as as a homicide investigation to be honest... because of what happened and we think somebody else intentionally caused the act.

key_moments.description: The father attempts to delay the arrest, leading to a standoff.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: I'd rather just get an appointment and bring him down tomorrow... No, I don't think so.

key_moments.description: Father instructs his son to only say 'lawyer'.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: You don't say a word to anybody. The only word you know is lawyer.

key_moments.description: Joe Kanig invokes his right to counsel immediately.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: I'd prefer to have a lawyer with me.

key_moments.description: Zach claims he was home watching Rick and Morty, but cannot name a character.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: Finished Rick and Morty... Tell me the name of the character... I have no idea.

key_moments.description: Investigator reveals the murder charge to Zach.
key_moments.transcript_excerpt: You've been implicated in throwing the rock that hit Alexa Martell in the head and killed her.

content_pitch: A seemingly tragic car accident is rapidly reclassified as a homicide when deputies connect it to a reckless rock-throwing spree miles away. The investigation quickly pivots to intense interrogations where shifting alibis and immediate lawyer requests expose a group of teenagers trying to cover up their fatal mistake. With a perfect cold_open structure and a winning artifact combination of bodycam, interrogation, and documents, this case delivers high-stakes reveals and undeniable contradictions that will drive massive viewer retention.
